Milano · Winter Collection

A Palace of
Liquid Gold

Behind a Haussmannian arched window, where winter mist meets the warmth of roasted Arabica — the Golden Cup awaits its court.

Discover the Royal Menu Our Heritage
La Maison

Where Renaissance
Meets the Boulevard

Born beneath the gilded arcades of Milan and dressed in the classicism of old Paris, Golden Cup is not a café — it is a chamber of ceremony. Dark walnut panels absorb the murmur of the city; gold filigree catches the last amber light of a winter afternoon.

Every cup is composed like a sonata: single-origin Arabica, roasted in-house to a velvet darkness, extracted under chandelier light, and served upon marble that remembers a century of conversation.

18hSlow Steep
1:1Golden Ratio
N°1Via Montenapoleone
The Royal Menu

Four Courts of Indulgence

Each creation is photographed in its natural habitat — chiaroscuro light, marble, and the hush of falling snow beyond the glass.

Les Essences

The Ceremony

The Golden Ritual

Acte I

The Sourcing

Rare micro-lots of Arabica, chosen by our Maestro Torrefattore from volcanic terroirs, arrive in Milan under seal.

Acte II

The Roast

Fire and patience. Small batches darkened to velvet, releasing notes of cacao, dried fig, and burnished caramel.

Acte III

The Service

Poured into gold-rimmed porcelain, presented on marble, and accompanied — always — by silence, then delight.

Golden Cup
